summary: Make the final cleanroom walkthrough exercise the exact application payload extracted from the signed installer instead of requiring non-reproducible cross-machine PE byte identity.

externalInputs.purpose: Bound the correction to the successful final-main CI run and its signed Windows setup, packaged-application identity, lifecycle, and batch artifacts.
Consumed the protected review finding that the metadata label had accidentally replaced the real local source-build path.

providedInputs.label: Owner-provided ScadMill functional specification v0.6 with amendments

decisions.decision: Retain one locked clean source build as provenance, but extract the application from the exact signed installer, require its SHA-256 to equal the exact-CI packaged payload hash, and use those shipped bytes for the isolated Sandbox walkthrough.
decisions.rationale: The previous cross-machine byte-equality check failed before Sandbox even though source, lockfiles, and Rust 1.96.0 matched. Direct PE inspection established the exact CI payload used MSVC linker 14.51 while the host clean build used 14.44, changing section layout, executable size, timestamp, and hash. A signed-payload hash check is stable across hosts and tests the artifact users receive without weakening source or CI binding.

testEvidence.criterion: The cleanroom walkthrough runs the exact application payload from the signed installer and fails closed on any payload hash mismatch.
testEvidence.test: Require signed-payload extraction, packaged SHA-256 binding, shipped-byte source metadata, and M5/M6 identity checks.

red.outcome: RED: 4 tests failed because the wrapper still staged a local rebuild, the source metadata still named the source-build path, and M5/M6 still compared against the exact-CI built hash.

green.outcome: GREEN: 2 files and 60 tests passed after requiring exact signed-payload extraction and packaged-hash binding.

testEvidence.criterion: The retained clean source build resolves from its real Tauri target independently from the signed-payload metadata label.
testEvidence.test: Require separate source-build and staged signed-payload paths in the wrapper contract.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/packaged-desktop-evidence.test.ts -t "fails closed before staging unless a clean HEAD is built at the canonical target" --reporter=default
red.outcome: RED: the focused test failed because no independent sourceBuiltApplication path existed; the wrapper incorrectly resolved the local build from the signed-payload metadata label.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/packaged-desktop-evidence.test.ts -t "fails closed before staging unless a clean HEAD is built at the canonical target" --reporter=default
green.outcome: GREEN: the focused regression passed after separating the real Tauri source-build target from the exact signed-payload staging identity.

nearMisses.sought: Treat byte-identical Windows rebuilds on different hosted and local MSVC installations as a release prerequisite.
nearMisses.confirmation: Rejected after the fail-closed cleanroom gate exposed linker 14.51 versus 14.44 PE output differences. The replacement verifies the exact signed payload rather than weakening the comparison or accepting a locally rebuilt substitute.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
